name: Hyderabadi Chicken Biryani
description: Authentic 'Dum' style chicken biryani from Hyderabad. Layers of fragrant basmati rice and succulent chicken, marinated in aromatic spices and slow-cooked to perfection.
instructions: Marinate chicken with yogurt and spices for 4 hours.
Par-boil rice with whole spices until 70% done.
Layer marinated chicken and par-boiled rice in a heavy pot.
Top with fried onions, mint, and saffron milk.
Seal with dough and cook on low heat (dum) for 45 minutes.
suggestions: Serve with Mirchi Ka Salan and Raita.

name: Paneer Butter Masala
description: A rich and creamy North Indian favorite. Soft paneer cubes in a velvety tomato-based gravy enriched with butter and cream.
instructions: Puree tomatoes and cashews together.
Saute ginger-garlic paste in butter.
Add puree and cook until oil separates.
Mix in paneer cubes and simmer with cream.
Garnish with fresh coriander.

name: Masala Dosa
description: The quintessential South Indian breakfast. Crispy golden crepes stuffed with a savory, spiced potato masala.
instructions: Prepare potato masala by sautéing mashed potatoes with spices.
Spread dosa batter thinly on a hot griddle.
Drizzle ghee and cook until golden brown.
Place potato stuffing and fold.
Serve hot with coconut chutney and sambar.
suggestions: Serve with a dollop of butter on top.

name: Chole Bhature
description: Hearty Punjabi classic. Spicy chickpeas (chole) paired with soft, fluffy deep-fried leavened bread (bhature).
instructions: Soak chickpeas overnight and pressure cook until soft.
Prepare a spicy onion-tomato gravy with chole masala.
Knead bhatura dough with yogurt and let it rest.
Roll out bhaturas and deep fry until puffed.
Serve hot with pickles and onion rings.
suggestions: Add a tea bag while boiling chole for deep color.

name: Vada Pav
description: Mumbai's iconic street food. A spicy potato fritter (vada) sandwiched in a soft bun (pav) with zesty chutneys.
instructions: Make potato balls seasoned with garlic and chilies.
Dip balls in besan batter and deep fry until golden.
Apply spicy garlic chutney inside the pav.
Place the vada and press gently.
Serve with fried green chilies.
suggestions: The dry garlic chutney is essential for the kick!

name: Palak Paneer
description: A healthy and delicious spinach-based dish. Freshly pureed spinach cooked with spices and soft paneer cubes.
instructions: Blanch spinach and puree into a smooth paste.
Saute lots of garlic and ginger in butter.
Add spinach paste and cook for 5 minutes.
Add paneer cubes and salt. Simmer briefly.
Finish with a dash of cream.
suggestions: Do not overcook the spinach to keep its bright color.

name: Chicken Tikka Masala
description: Grilled marinated chicken chunks in a spicy, creamy tomato-based sauce. A global favorite of Indian cuisine.
instructions: Grill marinated chicken tikka pieces first.
Prepare a thick onion-tomato gravy with tandoori spices.
Add the grilled chicken to the gravy.
Simmer with cream and kasuri methi.
Serve hot with naan.
suggestions: Smoky flavor from grilling is the highlight.

name: Dal Makhani
description: Creamy black lentils slow-cooked overnight. A rich Punjabi delicacy made with butter, cream, and aromatic spices.
instructions: Soak dal overnight and pressure cook until very soft.
Simmer the dal with tomato puree and ginger-garlic paste for 2 hours.
Mash some lentils for that thick, velvety texture.
Add butter and cream. Simmer for another 20 minutes.
Serve with a dollop of white butter.
suggestions: The longer it simmers, the better it tastes.

name: Pav Bhaji
description: A buttery vegetable mash served with toasted pav. Mumbai's favorite street-side snack that's as colorful as it is tasty.
instructions: Boil and mash vegetables (potatoes, peas, carrots).
Cook with pav bhaji masala, onions, and tomatoes in lots of butter.
Mash while cooking on a flat tawa for authentic texture.
Toast pav with butter and chili powder.
Serve with onions, lemon, and extra butter.
suggestions: Squeeze fresh lemon for that zingy finish.

name: Gajar Ka Halwa
description: A classic Indian winter dessert. Grated carrots slow-cooked with milk, sugar, and ghee, garnished with dry fruits.
instructions: Grate carrots and cook in milk until it evaporates.
Add sugar and ghee. Saute until it turns dark orange.
Add cardamom powder and chopped nuts.
Serve warm.
suggestions: Use juicy red carrots for the best results.

name: Dhokla
description: Soft and spongy steamed snack made from fermented chickpea flour. A Gujarati favorite served with green chutney.
instructions: Mix besan, yogurt, and water to make a smooth batter. Ferment for 4 hours.
Steam the batter in a greased plate for 15-20 minutes.
Prepare a tempering of mustard seeds and curry leaves in oil.
Pour tempering over the steamed dhokla and cut into squares.
Serve with spicy green chutney.
suggestions: Add a pinch of fruit salt just before steaming for extra fluffiness.

name: Aloo Paratha
description: Popular North Indian flatbread stuffed with a spicy potato filling. Served hot with butter, yogurt, and pickles.
instructions: Boil and mash potatoes. Mix with chopped onions and spices.
Knead a soft dough with wheat flour and water.
Stuff a small portion of potato filling into a dough ball.
Roll out into a flatbread and cook on a griddle with ghee.
Serve hot with a dollop of butter.
suggestions: Don't overstuff the paratha to prevent tearing while rolling.
